title: Correlate k6 results with Grafana observability

blocks.content:
Welcome to the learning path that walks you from a Grafana Cloud k6 spike test through metrics, logs, and traces so you can judge whether a passing test really means the system is ready.

You follow the same time window in every step. That anchor is how performance engineers connect load-generator numbers to what customers experience in different regions.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Run a controlled spike test in Grafana Cloud k6 and capture a UTC anchor time for analysis
- Compare k6 latency with synthetic probe metrics in Prometheus
- Classify errors and timing from Loki logs for the demo service
- Locate slow and error spans in Tempo and relate them to end-to-end latency
- Draft a short postmortem narrative that ties the signals together

## Before you begin

Before you start, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account/).
- Access to Grafana Cloud k6 and to **Explore** with Prometheus, Loki, and Tempo data sources connected (demo stacks often ship prefilled sources; names may differ from the examples in the steps).
- Enough familiarity to paste and run a small k6 script. If you need a primer first, complete [Run your first k6 performance test](/docs/learning-paths/run-first-k6-test/).
- About 45–60 minutes of focused time.

## Troubleshooting

If a query returns no data, widen the time range a few minutes on each side of your test window and confirm you selected the correct data source. Synthetic probes and log labels vary by environment; adjust label filters to match your stack while keeping the same investigation order.

## More to explore

Optional deep dives appear at the end of the path. You can pause after any milestone—your notes and UTC anchor carry forward.
